goal: Track prices on things you care about, across categories, and get pinged on drops.

screens.name: Watchlist
screens.purpose: All tracked items with current price and trend.

states.empty: 'Nothing on your radar yet' + Add CTA.
states.success: Row per item: thumbnail, current price, small sparkline trend, target price.
states.error: Item whose scrape has been failing repeatedly shows 'Price unavailable' rather than a stale/wrong number.

screens.name: Add to Watchlist
screens.purpose: User pastes a product URL and sets a target price.

states.loading: Validating URL / fetching initial price + product image.
states.success: Preview card (image, name, current price) + target price stepper before confirming.
states.error: Unsupported site -> clear 'We can't track this site yet' rather than a silent failure.

screens.name: Price History
screens.purpose: Chart of price over time for one item.

states.success: Line chart of priceHistory[], lowest/highest markers, edit target price inline.

screens.name: Price Drop Push
screens.purpose: Push notification when a tracked item hits target price.

states.success: Deep links straight to price_history_detail for that item.

userFlow: User pastes a product URL (e.g. a charger listing) into add_watch_item.
Scheduled Cloud Function checks the price periodically in the background.
Price drops below target -> push notification -> price_drop_alert -> deep link to price_history_detail.
User taps through to the retailer to buy.
edgeCases: Retailer redesigns their page and the parser breaks -> item flips to 'Price unavailable' state, doesn't show a stale or fabricated price.
Unsupported/unknown site -> explicit rejection at add time, not a silent no-op.
Same item tracked at two different URLs -> no dedup attempted in v1, treated as two separate watch entries.
Terms-of-Service exposure — scraping a retailer's page may violate their ToS; this is a legal/product-risk item, not just an engineering edge case.
notes: v1 is a single-URL watchlist, not an autonomous cross-vertical aggregator. Each add_watch_item is one specific product the user explicitly chose to track — that scopes both the engineering effort and the ToS risk.
